tool_input.content:
# Fork divergence — RaggioS/jcode

This is a personal fork of [1jehuang/jcode](https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode) used as the sole
local-model coding harness driving Ollama `gemma4:12b` on macOS. It tracks upstream `master` and carries
a small set of changes on the `feat/local-gemma4-macos` branch.

## Carried patches (vs upstream)

1. **macOS image paste on non-English locales** — `crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/helpers.rs`,
   `crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/input.rs`.
   - `clipboard_image()`: AppleScript-ObjC reader → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`). The AppleScript
     dialect failed to parse on non-English system locales (Italian raised `-2741 "found plural class name"`),
     so image paste silently returned nothing. JXA syntax is locale-independent.
   - `handle_paste()`: when a bracketed paste delivers **empty text** (how macOS / the VS Code integrated
     terminal report Cmd+V for an image-only clipboard), read the clipboard image and attach it. Safe only
     for empty text — non-empty pastes keep the upstream Wayland multi-MIME guard.
   - Result: Cmd+V (and Ctrl+V) attach screenshots in the VS Code terminal on macOS-Italian. Good
     upstream-PR candidate.

## Runtime configuration (NOT in this repo — machine-local, templated in `pocket-llm/jcode/`)

- **Thinking off** for gemma4: Ollama `/v1` honors top-level `reasoning_effort`. Set it via a named provider
  in `~/.jcode/config.toml` (`[providers.ollama-local] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`) or the
  `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` env in the launcher.
- **Telemetry off**: `DO_NOT_TRACK=1`.
- **Italian persona**: jcode loads `~/AGENTS.md` (global) + project `AGENTS.md` into the system prompt
  (`crates/jcode-base/src/prompt.rs`). The lean Italian persona lives there.

## Keeping in sync with upstream

```bash
git fetch upstream
git rebase upstream/master            # on feat/local-gemma4-macos
cargo build --release --bin jcode
scripts/install_release.sh
```

# SOUL — Pocket LLM (Hermes persona)

Copy to `~/.hermes/SOUL.md`. This is the always-loaded global persona — prefilled
on EVERY message, so it stays lean. Per-project rules and context go in a
`HERMES.md` at the project root, not here.

Reply in Italian by default, regardless of the working directory or which
project is open. Short, aseptic, concise, functional — speed over verbosity, no
filler. Answers stay brief by default: a few sentences or lines, the direct
result first; expand only when asked. No preamble, no recap, no padding. Prose
is minimal — but code stays complete, never truncated or shortened to save
words. Switch language only on the user's cue. Code, commands, paths and
identifiers stay in their original form. Keep Italian orthography correct —
proper accents and diacritics (à è é ì ò ù), never ASCII substitutes.

Grounding: read the files and use tools to check before acting; never invent
file contents, function names, or APIs. When unsure, inspect or say so rather
than guess.

Work: explore before writing; change only the lines asked for; no placeholders
or TODOs; reuse over duplication; verify/build before closing; servers on
127.0.0.1.

Safety: destructive actions (rm, overwrite, push) need confirmation first;
operate only inside the working directory.

# Pocket LLM — Hermes config overrides for a LOCAL Ollama model (Gemma).
# Merge these keys into ~/.hermes/config.yaml (created by the Hermes installer).
# Only the keys below need changing from the defaults.

model:
  default: gemma4:12b                 # the Ollama model tag (see `ollama list`)
  provider: custom                    # Hermes treats a local Ollama OpenAI endpoint as a "custom" provider
  api_key: ollama                     # any non-empty value; Ollama ignores it
  base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1 # local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint (note the /v1)

# Stream tokens live in the panel (feels far faster on a local 12B).
streaming:
  enabled: true

# Thinking control. gemma4 is a hybrid-thinking model; on a 16GB machine long
# reasoning kills responsiveness, so default to OFF. The custom-provider patch
# (hermes/custom_provider_patched.py + scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh) maps
# this onto Ollama's reasoning_effort so the toggle actually takes effect:
#   none -> thinking OFF (fast)   ·   low|medium|high -> thinking ON at that level
# Flip it live in the TUI with /reasoning none|low|medium|high.
agent:
  reasoning_effort: none

# Pocket LLM — Hermes .env entries for LOCAL Ollama.
# Append these to ~/.hermes/.env (the dummy key just satisfies the OpenAI client;
# local Ollama ignores it). Do NOT commit your real ~/.hermes/.env.

OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama
OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1

# pocket-llm

Run a capable local AI coding agent directly in VS Code — styled like **Claude Code** — using
**[Hermes Agent](https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent)** (NousResearch) driven by
**Ollama** with **Gemma 4 12B** (or any local model). Fully offline, zero API tokens.

This repository contains:
1. **`vscode-extension/`** — a one-click **launcher** (status-bar button). It manages the Ollama
   server lifecycle (auto-start, keep-warm, RAM-aware context length), pre-warms the model, then
   opens the native **Hermes TUI** in a terminal docked as a parallel editor column — beside your
   code, not in the bottom panel.
2. **`hermes/`** — our Hermes configuration for local Gemma (model/provider/streaming overrides,
   trimmed tools/skills, persona). Drop these into `~/.hermes/` after installing Hermes. See
   `vscode-extension/HERMES.md` for the prefill-trimming details.

---

## Why this architecture

* **No swap / no "stops responding"**: the extension starts Ollama with a RAM-aware
  `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (≥32GB → 32768, ≥16GB → 16384, else 8192) so a 12B (~8GB) plus its KV
  cache fits unified memory. It also sets `OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m` so the model stays warm (no
  ~60s cold-load every turn).
* **Hermes does the agent work**: tools, file edits, memory, skills — we don't reinvent an agent
  loop. Hermes also compresses its own context, which keeps the prefill smaller than a repo-map
  dump on a small local model.
* **Native Hermes TUI, one click**: the launcher starts/ warms everything and drops you straight
  into Hermes in its own Terminal window — no manual `ollama serve`, no env juggling.
* **Small prefill on a small model**: heavy tools are disabled and bundled skills removed from the
  profile so per-turn prefill stays tiny (warm turns ~8s on a 16GB Mac). All re-activatable —
  see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`.

---

## Installation & setup

### 1. Prerequisites
- **Ollama** (`brew install ollama`), with a model pulled:
  ```bash
  ollama pull gemma4:12b
  ```
- **Hermes Agent** — official installer (user-level, no sudo; `--skip-browser` avoids the
  heavy Chromium download, `--skip-setup` skips the interactive wizard):
  ```bash
  curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash -s -- --skip-browser --skip-setup
  source ~/.zshrc   # reload PATH so `hermes` is found
  ```

### 2. Point Hermes at local Ollama (our config)
The Hermes installer creates `~/.hermes/{config.yaml,.env,SOUL.md}`. Apply our overrides:
- Merge `hermes/config.snippet.yaml` into `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (sets provider `custom`,
  `base_url` `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, `model.default` `gemma4:12b`, streaming on, thinking off).
- Append `hermes/env.sample` to `~/.hermes/.env`:
  ```bash
  cat hermes/env.sample >> ~/.hermes/.env
  ```
- Optionally copy our persona: `cp hermes/SOUL.md ~/.hermes/SOUL.md`.

#### Enable the thinking toggle (gemma4)
gemma4 is a hybrid-thinking model. Hermes talks to Ollama over the OpenAI `/v1` endpoint, which
ignores the native `think` flag but (Ollama ≥ 0.30.x) honors `reasoning_effort`. A small patch to
Hermes' custom-provider plugin makes `/reasoning none|low|medium|high` actually take effect:
```bash
bash scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh
```
Run it once now, and again after every `hermes update` (the update overwrites Hermes' source tree,
dropping the patch). The script is idempotent and backs up the live plugin before patching. Then in
the TUI: `none` = thinking off (fast), `low|medium|high` = thinking on at that level.

Verify it talks to local Gemma:
```bash
hermes chat -q "Scrivi una funzione python che somma due numeri. Solo codice."
```
(First call is slow — cold model load. Warm calls are ~10-15s on a 16GB Mac.)

### 3. Install the VS Code extension
```bash
cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher
# VS Code: Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P → "Developer: Reload Window"
```

---

## How to use

1. Open a workspace folder in VS Code.
2. Click **$(rocket) Hermes** in the status bar (or run the `Avvia Hermes (Pocket LLM)` command).
3. The launcher auto-starts Ollama (warm, RAM-sized context), pre-warms the model, and opens a
   terminal as a **parallel editor column** (beside your code) with the local-Ollama env set.
4. Hermes' native TUI is running there: type your task and press **Enter**. Hermes thinks, edits
   files in the workspace, and runs commands via its own tools.
5. Done for now? Run `Pocket LLM: ferma server Ollama` to free the RAM.

> Note: we do **not** ship models. Pull them with `ollama pull`. Hermes lives in `~/.hermes`
> (like any installed CLI); this repo carries only our config + the VS Code panel.

---

## Tuning knobs
- `~/.hermes/config.yaml` — `model.default` (any local Ollama tag), `agent.reasoning_effort`
  (`none`/`low`/`medium`/`high` — needs the thinking patch above), `streaming`, `compression`,
  `memory`. Thinking off + streaming = snappiest on a local 12B.
- Tools / skills — disabled by default to shrink prefill; re-enable on demand
  (`hermes tools enable …`, `hermes skills opt-in`). See `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`.
- Context length / keep-alive are set automatically by the extension when it launches Ollama.

file.content:
---
name: pocket-llm
description: Local offline AI coding agent in VS Code — Hermes Agent + Ollama (Gemma 4 12B). Pivoted from the old Qwen/llama-cpp multimodal chat.
metadata:
  type: project
---
# CURRENT STATE (2026-06-17) — supersedes the legacy notes below

pocket-llm is now a **local coding agent**, not the old multimodal chat. Stack:
- **Backend**: Ollama (`brew`, `:11434`), model **gemma4:12b** (~7.6GB). Old Qwen3-VL/llama-server
  stack abandoned. Custom `gemma4-32k` model deleted.
- **Agent**: **Hermes Agent** (NousResearch, MIT) installed at `~/.hermes` via official
  `curl … nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash --skip-browser --skip-setup`. CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes`.
  Replaced **Aider** (uv tool `aider-chat`, now UNINSTALLED + all configs removed).
- **UI = launcher, NOT a webview**: `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/` status-bar button (`localAi.launch`)
  starts/pre-warms Ollama, then opens the native `hermes` TUI in a terminal docked as a **parallel
  editor column** — `createTerminal({ location: { viewColumn: vscode.ViewColumn.Beside } })`, beside the
  editor, NOT the bottom panel, NOT an external window (osascript was tried and reverted). The old custom
  streaming webview (`media/webview.js`) is gone. Tools/skills trimmed for small prefill → warm ~8s/turn
  (see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`; all re-activatable).
- **Deploy gotcha**: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` NESTS when the dest
  dir already exists (makes `.../local-ai-launcher/vscode-extension/`) → VS Code keeps loading the stale
  top-level `extension.js`. Deploy by copying CONTENTS: `cp -R vscode-extension/. ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`
  (trailing `/.`), then Reload Window.
- **Hermes config for local Ollama** (`~/.hermes/`): `config.yaml` model.provider=ollama,
  base_url=`http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, model.default=gemma4:12b, streaming on, reasoning_effort low.
  `.env` needs `OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama` + `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`. Our copies
  committed in `pocket-llm/hermes/` (config.snippet.yaml, env.sample, SOUL.md).
- **CRITICAL perf fix**: agent "stops responding" was `num_ctx 32768` on 16GB (12B+KV swaps).
  The extension now launches Ollama with **RAM-aware `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`** (≥32GB→32768,
  ≥16GB→16384, else 8192) + **`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`** (no cold-load each turn). Direct Ollama is
  fast (tiny prompt); any agent harness sends a big prefill → that was the slowness, NOT the tool.
  Hermes warm ≈ 8s/simple query on 17GB; cold ≈ 28-89s (one-time model load).
- **Perf knobs added (2026-06-18)**: launcher also sets **`OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1` + `OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE=q8_0`**
  → ~half KV-cache RAM, less swap as the 16k ctx fills (applies on next launcher-started Ollama; stop+relaunch to activate).
  Hermes config: `display.interface: tui`, `model_catalog.enabled:false` (offline), `security.tirith_enabled:false`
  (binary not installed → inert), `stt.enabled:false` (external STT used), `file_read_max_chars:20000`,
  `tool_output.max_bytes:16000` (keep 16k ctx lean → fewer compressions long-session). LSP KEPT on (diagnostics useful).
  Base prefill (system prompt ~11KB cached + tool schemas) is at the floor; the 12B is the real speed ceiling.
- **Prefill gotcha**: Hermes loads ONE cwd context file by priority HERMES.md>AGENTS.md>CLAUDE.md ([[hermes-agent-docs]]).
  Launching Hermes from a project subdir that has a `CLAUDE.md` (e.g. pocket-llm's symlink, 8KB) loads it → fat prefill.
  The launcher uses cwd=workspace ROOT, where `HERMES.md` (tuned, ~1.2KB) wins — so the normal button flow is fine.
- **Runtimes live in system dirs** (Hermes `~/.hermes`, Ollama models `~/.ollama`), like uv/ollama;
  only code+config+README are under `$WS/pocket-llm`.
- **Model = gemma4:12b — qwen3-vl:8b evaluated & REJECTED (2026-06-23), don't redo**: deep-researched a
  multimodal replacement. (a) Default `qwen3-vl:8b` is the THINKING variant (`RENDERER qwen3-vl-thinking`),
  reasoning NOT disable-able via Ollama (`think:false` / `/no_think` ignored) → 20-231s per "Ciao", unusable
  interactive on 16GB. (b) `qwen3-vl:8b-instruct` is non-thinking, fast (warm ~5s, "Ciao" 4.6s), multimodal
  (vision verified, "Red"), 6.1GB — WORKS, but **gemma4-12B beats it on every directly-comparable benchmark**:
  MMLU-Pro 77.2 vs 71.6, GPQA Diamond 78.8 vs 61.9, **LiveCodeBench v6 72.0 vs 39.3**; gemma4 also newer
  (Jun 2026, official Google model card) + has audio. Both non-thinking (gemma4 has no CoT either). Conclusion:
  **stay on gemma4:12b**. The earlier "qwen3-vl newer+stronger" premise was vs gemma-**3** (60.6/40.9) by mistake;
  vs the installed gemma-**4** it's a downgrade. Hermes model-switch mechanics → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
- **gemma4 thinking toggle — SOLVED 2026-06-23** (was thought unsuppressible): Ollama ≥0.30.x `/v1` honors
  `reasoning_effort` (`none`=off, `low`/`medium`/`high`=on) though it ignores `think`. Hermes' custom-provider
  plugin only sent `think:false` → ineffective; patched it to also emit `reasoning_effort`. Now `/reasoning none`
  = OFF, `/reasoning low|medium|high` = ON at level. SOURCE patch → re-apply after `hermes update`. Full detail
  + re-apply steps → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
- **Speed is decode-bound, NOT prefill-bound (measured 2026-06-24)**: warm gemma4:12b on M5 16GB →
  prompt-eval **~7300 tok/s** (even 6k-token system prompt = <1s), generation **7.6 tok/s** (sequential).
  So latency ≈ output-tokens × 0.13s; a ~170-tok answer = ~22s. **Prefill trimming / prompt-cache preheat give
  ~0 gain** — prefill already near-free; Ollama already reuses the static prefix. The ONE preheat that matters is
  keeping weights in RAM (`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`, set by the launcher) → kills the 20-30s cold-load. Real levers
  left: (1) fewer output tokens — DONE via stronger brevity in SOUL (≈20-25s typical, [[hermes-agent-docs]]);
  (2) a faster model. Thinking ON scales time with task (more reasoning tokens = more seconds), by design.
- **Fast lane (7-8B) — DEFERRED by user 2026-06-24, keep gemma4:12b default**: idea = add a small model as a
  SECOND lane switchable via `/model` for quick coding, gemma4 stays default. Researched the quality gap: it's
  specialization vs generality, not simply "smaller=worse". A GENERIC 7-8B is clearly below a 12B on reasoning/
  broad knowledge/long-context/multimodal (user's instinct right). BUT a SPECIALIZED coder-7B (e.g.
  `qwen2.5-coder:7b`) closes/reverses the gap ON CODE — reports: matches 20B+ models, beats DS-Coder-33B,
  HumanEval-Instruct ~85% (Qwen2.5-Coder Technical Report, arxiv 2409.12186). Trade-off: a coder-7B loses gemma4's
  vision + general reasoning. So a fast lane is only worth it with a SPECIALIZED coder, for quick code tasks only.
  Not built (current 20-25s deemed fine); revisit only if quick-turn latency annoys.
- **Sampling tuned — temperature 0.6 / top_p 0.9 (2026-06-24, PR #14)**: gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0
  (too high → rambly, less deterministic code). Set a single all-purpose middle ground (0.6) — tighter/correct for
  code, still flexible for chat — via the custom-provider plugin as TOP-LEVEL OpenAI params (Ollama /v1 honors
  top-level temperature, ignores `options.temperature`; determinism-verified). User declined a lower 0.3 ("deve
  servire per tutto") and a separate Modelfile/model. Re-applied by the reapply script after `hermes update`.
  Details → [[hermes-agent-docs]].

---
# pocket-llm (legacy notes — Qwen multimodal chat, superseded)

Learning PoC: prove a *small dense* model at *low quant* runs on modest hardware,
zero API tokens. Private repo `github.com/RaggioS/pocket-llm`, git-ignored subfolder
in `$WS/pocket-llm`, own git remote (not part of claude-config). Branch `main`.

Born from the "quantize DeepSeek-V4 to 8GB" misconception — that's physically
impossible (see [[distributed-inference-tools]]); the viable target is a small dense
model. Sibling/learning-project of [[subscription-sharing]].

## Shared-config inheritance pattern (reusable for all future projects)

Subfolder gets shared rules/hooks/skills via **symlinks**, keeps its own code isolated:
- `pocket-llm/.claude -> ../.claude` (symlink) · `pocket-llm/CLAUDE.md -> ../CLAUDE.md` (symlink)
- project `.gitignore` lists `.claude` + `CLAUDE.md` → symlinks NOT committed to the
  project repo (shared config stays owned by claude-config, no duplication/drift).
- parent claude-config `.gitignore` (ignore-all + whitelist) already excludes the
  whole project folder → `git check-ignore pocket-llm` ✓; project has own remote.
- This is the same precedent as subscription-sharing. Replicate for any new repo.

## Stack & layout

- Python `>=3.9`, `uv` project (hatchling build). Deps: `llama-cpp-python>=0.3`,
  `huggingface-hub>=0.24`. Entry point `pocket-llm = pocket_llm.cli:main`.
- **Invoke with `uv run python -m pocket_llm`** (NOT the `pocket-llm` console script).
  Root cause: hatchling editable-install `.pth` (absolute path) is silently NOT honored by
  CPython 3.14's `site.py` when the project path contains a space (`AI Projects`) → console
  script raises `ModuleNotFoundError`. `uv run python -c` works (uv injects path) but the
  installed bin doesn't. `python -m` from repo root puts CWD on `sys.path[0]` → flat-layout
  package imports directly, no editable/.pth needed. `pocket_llm/__main__.py` enables it.
  Layout is flat (`pocket_llm/` at root, NOT `src/`).
- `pocket_llm/engine.py`: `Engine` wraps `llama_cpp.Llama`; `stream()` yields
  chat chunks, `generate()` returns `GenMetrics` (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens≈chunks,
  ttft_s, total_s, peak_rss_mb via `ru_maxrss`; `tokens_per_s` property). `n_gpu_layers=-1`
  = all layers on Metal.
- `pocket_llm/cli.py`: argparse — one-shot `pocket-llm "prompt"` or REPL; footer
  `[ N tok | X tok/s | TTFT ms | peak RAM MB ]` to stderr.
- `scripts/download_model.py`: `hf_hub_download` → `./models/`. Default
  `unsloth/Qwen3.5-4B-GGUF :: Qwen3.5-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf` (~2.6GB). Default `--n-ctx` 16384
  (Qwen3.5 = 262K native / 1M via YaRN, but KV cache ~140KB/tok caps usable ctx ~16-32K on 8GB).
- `models/` + `*.gguf` gitignored (never commit weights).

## Status

- **Verified end-to-end** on the user's M-series Mac: `uv sync` built `llama-cpp-python==0.3.29`
  (Metal). Default now **Qwen3.5-4B Q4_K_M** (~2.6GB, no 404); old Qwen2.5-3B gguf deleted.
  Real numbers via `python -m pocket_llm` @ n_ctx 16384: **Qwen3.5-4B ~35.8 tok/s, peak RAM
  ~3.2 GB, TTFT ~270 ms** (earlier Qwen2.5-3B was ~57–58 tok/s / ~2.3GB). Well under 8GB.
  100% free (Apache-2.0 weights, MIT llama.cpp, local/offline, zero API tokens).
- `completion_tokens` counts stream chunks (approx tokens).

## CCR integration (no new CLI)

`pocket_llm/serve.py` → `uv run python -m pocket_llm.serve` exposes the model over an
**OpenAI-compatible** API (`127.0.0.1:8080/v1`, model id `qwen3.5-4b-local`), wrapping
`llama-cpp-python[server]`. Registered as the `local` provider in CCR (see [[ccr-openrouter]]). CCR routes
**per-request** on the model field → a local session (`qwen` = `command ccr code
--model "local,qwen3.5-4b-local"`) runs in PARALLEL with cloud `ccr code`, no global
switch / no 2nd instance. Files isolated per session via `work` (git worktree).
Verified: `/v1/chat/completions` returns a completion. NOT added to the `fallback-models`
transformer (would overwrite the model id). Claude Code (official) can't use local models
directly — CCR is the only integration point.

**End-to-end CCR routing VERIFIED** (2026-06-15): request to CCR's Anthropic endpoint
(`127.0.0.1:3456/v1/messages`) with `Router.default='local,qwen3.5-4b-local'` → routed
through local provider → llama server → reply `pong`, response `model=qwen3.5-4b-local`.
Default restored to OpenRouter afterwards (local is opt-in via `ccr-local`).

## Serve stability — DO NOT re-enable flash_attn/cache/big batch

Qwen3.5-4B is a **hybrid SSM/attention** model. `--flash_attn true` triggers a Metal
kernel-compilation storm → server crashes (empty reply, port held by orphan
`llama_cpp.server` child). `--n_batch 2048 + --cache true` together also crash it.
**Verified-stable serve config**: `n_ctx 32768`, `n_batch 1024`, no flash_attn, no cache
(committed in `serve.py`, exposes `--n-batch`; 2048 only crashes WITH flash+cache). Bench:
floor latency 0.9s, 7019-token prefill ~40s (~176 tok/s), server stays UP. Prefill scales
linearly → Claude-Code's ~26k-token/turn context ≈ ~150s/turn (4B prefill is physics, not a
bug). The earlier "non risponde quasi mai" was the flash/cache crash + content-array 500
([[ccr-openrouter]] `local-content-fix`), now both fixed. Kill BOTH on stop:
`pkill -f pocket_llm.serve; pkill -f llama_cpp.server`.

**Two UX gotchas (not server bugs):** (1) llama-cpp-python serves ONE request at a time —
a big in-flight turn blocks new ones; Claude Code's UI interrupt does NOT cancel server-side
inference (HTTP disconnect ignored), so a stuck turn keeps the slot busy → restart server to
clear it. (2) Biggest user-side speed lever is `/effort low` in the session (medium makes a
4B emit long reasoning → minutes). RAM is NOT a lever: model is 3.2GB, fully on Metal,
compute-bound not memory-bound — adding RAM does nothing. Local = offline/simple; fast
interactive work = cloud.

## DECISION (2026-06-15): local = offline/experiment only; interactive = cloud

Settled after the user hit ~2min/turn on a plain "ciao" in Claude Code. Proof: the RAW
model answers "ciao" in **0.36s**; the same "ciao" via Claude Code/CCR carries ~26k tokens
of system+tools harness → ~150s prefill, and the hybrid SSM can't cache it. No param fixes
this — it's structural. **User chose: keep local installed for offline/test, do all
interactive work on cloud (Claude Code Opus + CCR→OpenRouter, already the default).** Do NOT
re-pitch local-in-Claude-Code as "fast"; it is reliable, not fast. Local server is normally
STOPPED (relaunch via the VS Code extension only when offline). A stuck llama_cpp.server
ignores SIGTERM mid-Metal-compute → needs `kill -9`.

## VS Code extension = native chat webview (v0.0.6, model-agnostic)

**Lives in `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/`** (RaggioS/pocket-llm@main — MOVED here from
claude-config so the whole product is one repo; deploy: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` + Reload Window). Now v0.0.16. Big webview script is `vscode-extension/media/webview.js` (real file via asWebviewUri — avoids template-literal escaping). Features added: streaming SSE, stop-gen, multi-session panels (each launch = new panel/session), in-panel history overlay (open/delete), markdown + code blocks (copy/open-editor/save-file), personas + "about me" profile + temp/max_tokens (globalState), text/code file context, image input. Live copy `~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`.
Repointed from `ccr code --model local` (Claude Code harness, ~26k tok/turn, minutes) to a
**native webview chat** that POSTs DIRECTLY to `127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions` via
extension↔webview message-passing (no CCR, no harness) → ~2s replies, offline, zero token.
Button/status-bar `localAi.launch` opens the panel; auto-starts server + polls readiness;
stop kills both processes. **Model-agnostic**: UI says "Local AI", the model is only in
setting `localAi.modelId` (swap a future better local model with one setting, no rename).
Other settings: `serverUrl`, `serveDir`, `maxTokens` (512), `systemPrompt` (distilled house
rules — do NOT paste full CLAUDE.md/memories here, it re-bloats context and kills speed).
What CANNOT carry to a raw chat: hooks, skills, status-line (Claude Code machinery); only
CLAUDE.md/memory *content* can, as a short system prompt. Bump version to force VS Code reload.

## MULTIMODAL (2026-06-15): Qwen3-VL-8B via llama-server, text + images

Local chat is now **multimodal**. Model swapped from text-only Qwen3.5-4B to **Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct
Q4_K_M** (`models/qwen3-vl-8b/`, ~4.7GB + mmproj-F16 ~1.1GB). KEY: `llama-cpp-python` does NOT
support Qwen3-VL → serve via llama.cpp's native **`llama-server`** (`brew install llama.cpp`,
binary at `/opt/homebrew/bin/llama-server`, also `llama-mtmd-cli`). New wrapper
`pocket_llm/serve_vision.py` runs `llama-server -m <model> --mmproj <proj> -ngl 99 -c 8192 --jinja`,
OpenAI-compatible on :8080, model id `qwen3-vl-8b-local`. Accepts `image_url` content parts
(data URLs). Verified e2e: text ~1s/27 tok/s, image (red square → "Rosso") ~0.6s, server UP.
Extension (v0.0.9) starts `serve_vision`, panel has image input (attach 📎 / paste / drag-drop,
thumbnails, bubbles render images, history persists multimodal msgs). stopServer kills
`serve_vision`+`llama-server`. Old text path (`serve.py`, Qwen3.5-4B gguf) still on disk as
fallback, now superseded.

## Bench + UX polish (2026-06-15, panel v0.0.11)

Bench Qwen3-VL-8B (M-series, 17GB): RAM RSS **4.1GB**; gen **~25 tok/s** costante (corto=lungo);
TTFT testo corto 54–464ms (caldo ~50ms); prefill grande ~3700 tok = **TTFT 12.7s** (collo
bottiglia); immagine 64px 0.6s, immagine reale 1024×768 **4.5s**. **1 solo slot** → richieste
concorrenti serializzano. Panel ora: **streaming SSE** (postChatStream, token live), **bottone
Stop** (Invia→Stop rosso durante gen, aborta req, tiene parziale), **indicatore prefill pulsante**
(bolla "…" fade, copre l'attesa muta immagini grandi). maxTokens setting default 512.

## Status: COMPLETE & shipped

Repo `RaggioS/pocket-llm` (private). Full reproducible README (Requirements,
install-on-another-device, GPU backends, CCR integration + toggle, troubleshooting, tests),
LICENSE (MIT), smoke tests green (`uv run --extra dev pytest`). Engine + CLI + REPL + serve,
default Qwen3.5-4B Q4. CCR local provider live + e2e-verified. Nothing pending to ship.

## Roadmap (optional future)

Bench harness (sweep Q2/Q4/Q5/Q8 → tok/s vs RAM vs quality), "does it fit?" pre-flight
(file size + free RAM before load), CPU-only mode bench for low-end/Windows machines.
- **Richer Hermes prefill — DONE & validated 2026-06-23**: added a "Per-project quick facts" section to
  `HERMES.md` (run commands + key gotchas per project) so Hermes answers grounded questions without runtime
  discovery tool-calls. Measured: HERMES.md 1592→2477 B, +232 prompt tokens, **warm prefill +8ms** (172→181ms;
  prompt-cache absorbs it), cold +845ms once per session. Grounding verified: "how to run pocket-llm" →
  `uv run python -m pocket_llm`, no tools, top behavioral rules NOT diluted. Verdict: worth it, kept. Sweet
  spot reached — pushing further adds cold cost + dilution risk for little gain. Method for future tweaks:
  measure warm `prompt_eval_duration` x2 (2nd = cached) before/after; do NOT dump CLAUDE.md/memories (re-bloats).

**Triggers:** pocket-llm, quantized local LLM, Qwen 3B Q4, llama-cpp-python, 8GB LLM, GGUF, tok/s bench, local inference PoC
**Why:** new ongoing project outliving the task; documents the symlink shared-config pattern reused by future repos.
**How to apply:** next session on this repo, resume from roadmap; for a NEW separate project replicate the symlink+gitignore pattern above. Use `( cd pocket-llm && ... )` subshell per [[bash-cwd-hooks]].

# CURRENT STATE (2026-06-17) — supersedes the legacy notes below

pocket-llm is now a **local coding agent**, not the old multimodal chat. Stack:
- **Backend**: Ollama (`brew`, `:11434`), model **gemma4:12b** (~7.6GB). Old Qwen3-VL/llama-server
  stack abandoned. Custom `gemma4-32k` model deleted.
- **Agent**: **Hermes Agent** (NousResearch, MIT) installed at `~/.hermes` via official
  `curl … nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash --skip-browser --skip-setup`. CLI `~/.local/bin/hermes`.
  Replaced **Aider** (uv tool `aider-chat`, now UNINSTALLED + all configs removed).
- **UI = launcher, NOT a webview**: `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/` status-bar button (`localAi.launch`)
  starts/pre-warms Ollama, then opens the native `hermes` TUI in a terminal docked as a **parallel
  editor column** — `createTerminal({ location: { viewColumn: vscode.ViewColumn.Beside } })`, beside the
  editor, NOT the bottom panel, NOT an external window (osascript was tried and reverted). The old custom
  streaming webview (`media/webview.js`) is gone. Tools/skills trimmed for small prefill → warm ~8s/turn
  (see `vscode-extension/HERMES.md`; all re-activatable).
- **Deploy gotcha**: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` NESTS when the dest
  dir already exists (makes `.../local-ai-launcher/vscode-extension/`) → VS Code keeps loading the stale
  top-level `extension.js`. Deploy by copying CONTENTS: `cp -R vscode-extension/. ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`
  (trailing `/.`), then Reload Window.
- **Hermes config for local Ollama** (`~/.hermes/`): `config.yaml` model.provider=ollama,
  base_url=`http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`, model.default=gemma4:12b, streaming on, reasoning_effort low.
  `.env` needs `OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama` + `OLLAMA_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`. Our copies
  committed in `pocket-llm/hermes/` (config.snippet.yaml, env.sample, SOUL.md).
- **CRITICAL perf fix**: agent "stops responding" was `num_ctx 32768` on 16GB (12B+KV swaps).
  The extension now launches Ollama with **RAM-aware `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH`** (≥32GB→32768,
  ≥16GB→16384, else 8192) + **`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`** (no cold-load each turn). Direct Ollama is
  fast (tiny prompt); any agent harness sends a big prefill → that was the slowness, NOT the tool.
  Hermes warm ≈ 8s/simple query on 17GB; cold ≈ 28-89s (one-time model load).
- **Perf knobs added (2026-06-18)**: launcher also sets **`OLLAMA_FLASH_ATTENTION=1` + `OLLAMA_KV_CACHE_TYPE=q8_0`**
  → ~half KV-cache RAM, less swap as the 16k ctx fills (applies on next launcher-started Ollama; stop+relaunch to activate).
  Hermes config: `display.interface: tui`, `model_catalog.enabled:false` (offline), `security.tirith_enabled:false`
  (binary not installed → inert), `stt.enabled:false` (external STT used), `file_read_max_chars:20000`,
  `tool_output.max_bytes:16000` (keep 16k ctx lean → fewer compressions long-session). LSP KEPT on (diagnostics useful).
  Base prefill (system prompt ~11KB cached + tool schemas) is at the floor; the 12B is the real speed ceiling.
- **Prefill gotcha**: Hermes loads ONE cwd context file by priority HERMES.md>AGENTS.md>CLAUDE.md ([[hermes-agent-docs]]).
  Launching Hermes from a project subdir that has a `CLAUDE.md` (e.g. pocket-llm's symlink, 8KB) loads it → fat prefill.
  The launcher uses cwd=workspace ROOT, where `HERMES.md` (tuned, ~1.2KB) wins — so the normal button flow is fine.
- **Runtimes live in system dirs** (Hermes `~/.hermes`, Ollama models `~/.ollama`), like uv/ollama;
  only code+config+README are under `$WS/pocket-llm`.
- **Model = gemma4:12b — qwen3-vl:8b evaluated & REJECTED (2026-06-23), don't redo**: deep-researched a
  multimodal replacement. (a) Default `qwen3-vl:8b` is the THINKING variant (`RENDERER qwen3-vl-thinking`),
  reasoning NOT disable-able via Ollama (`think:false` / `/no_think` ignored) → 20-231s per "Ciao", unusable
  interactive on 16GB. (b) `qwen3-vl:8b-instruct` is non-thinking, fast (warm ~5s, "Ciao" 4.6s), multimodal
  (vision verified, "Red"), 6.1GB — WORKS, but **gemma4-12B beats it on every directly-comparable benchmark**:
  MMLU-Pro 77.2 vs 71.6, GPQA Diamond 78.8 vs 61.9, **LiveCodeBench v6 72.0 vs 39.3**; gemma4 also newer
  (Jun 2026, official Google model card) + has audio. Both non-thinking (gemma4 has no CoT either). Conclusion:
  **stay on gemma4:12b**. The earlier "qwen3-vl newer+stronger" premise was vs gemma-**3** (60.6/40.9) by mistake;
  vs the installed gemma-**4** it's a downgrade. Hermes model-switch mechanics → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
- **gemma4 thinking toggle — SOLVED 2026-06-23** (was thought unsuppressible): Ollama ≥0.30.x `/v1` honors
  `reasoning_effort` (`none`=off, `low`/`medium`/`high`=on) though it ignores `think`. Hermes' custom-provider
  plugin only sent `think:false` → ineffective; patched it to also emit `reasoning_effort`. Now `/reasoning none`
  = OFF, `/reasoning low|medium|high` = ON at level. SOURCE patch → re-apply after `hermes update`. Full detail
  + re-apply steps → [[hermes-agent-docs]].
- **Speed is decode-bound, NOT prefill-bound (measured 2026-06-24)**: warm gemma4:12b on M5 16GB →
  prompt-eval **~7300 tok/s** (even 6k-token system prompt = <1s), generation **7.6 tok/s** (sequential).
  So latency ≈ output-tokens × 0.13s; a ~170-tok answer = ~22s. **Prefill trimming / prompt-cache preheat give
  ~0 gain** — prefill already near-free; Ollama already reuses the static prefix. The ONE preheat that matters is
  keeping weights in RAM (`OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m`, set by the launcher) → kills the 20-30s cold-load. Real levers
  left: (1) fewer output tokens — DONE via stronger brevity in SOUL (≈20-25s typical, [[hermes-agent-docs]]);
  (2) a faster model. Thinking ON scales time with task (more reasoning tokens = more seconds), by design.
- **Fast lane (7-8B) — DEFERRED by user 2026-06-24, keep gemma4:12b default**: idea = add a small model as a
  SECOND lane switchable via `/model` for quick coding, gemma4 stays default. Researched the quality gap: it's
  specialization vs generality, not simply "smaller=worse". A GENERIC 7-8B is clearly below a 12B on reasoning/
  broad knowledge/long-context/multimodal (user's instinct right). BUT a SPECIALIZED coder-7B (e.g.
  `qwen2.5-coder:7b`) closes/reverses the gap ON CODE — reports: matches 20B+ models, beats DS-Coder-33B,
  HumanEval-Instruct ~85% (Qwen2.5-Coder Technical Report, arxiv 2409.12186). Trade-off: a coder-7B loses gemma4's
  vision + general reasoning. So a fast lane is only worth it with a SPECIALIZED coder, for quick code tasks only.
  Not built (current 20-25s deemed fine); revisit only if quick-turn latency annoys.
- **Sampling tuned — temperature 0.6 / top_p 0.9 (2026-06-24, PR #14)**: gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0
  (too high → rambly, less deterministic code). Set a single all-purpose middle ground (0.6) — tighter/correct for
  code, still flexible for chat — via the custom-provider plugin as TOP-LEVEL OpenAI params (Ollama /v1 honors
  top-level temperature, ignores `options.temperature`; determinism-verified). User declined a lower 0.3 ("deve
  servire per tutto") and a separate Modelfile/model. Re-applied by the reapply script after `hermes update`.
  Details → [[hermes-agent-docs]].

---
# pocket-llm (legacy notes — Qwen multimodal chat, superseded)

Learning PoC: prove a *small dense* model at *low quant* runs on modest hardware,
zero API tokens. Private repo `github.com/RaggioS/pocket-llm`, git-ignored subfolder
in `$WS/pocket-llm`, own git remote (not part of claude-config). Branch `main`.

Born from the "quantize DeepSeek-V4 to 8GB" misconception — that's physically
impossible (see [[distributed-inference-tools]]); the viable target is a small dense
model. Sibling/learning-project of [[subscription-sharing]].

## Shared-config inheritance pattern (reusable for all future projects)

Subfolder gets shared rules/hooks/skills via **symlinks**, keeps its own code isolated:
- `pocket-llm/.claude -> ../.claude` (symlink) · `pocket-llm/CLAUDE.md -> ../CLAUDE.md` (symlink)
- project `.gitignore` lists `.claude` + `CLAUDE.md` → symlinks NOT committed to the
  project repo (shared config stays owned by claude-config, no duplication/drift).
- parent claude-config `.gitignore` (ignore-all + whitelist) already excludes the
  whole project folder → `git check-ignore pocket-llm` ✓; project has own remote.
- This is the same precedent as subscription-sharing. Replicate for any new repo.

## Stack & layout

- Python `>=3.9`, `uv` project (hatchling build). Deps: `llama-cpp-python>=0.3`,
  `huggingface-hub>=0.24`. Entry point `pocket-llm = pocket_llm.cli:main`.
- **Invoke with `uv run python -m pocket_llm`** (NOT the `pocket-llm` console script).
  Root cause: hatchling editable-install `.pth` (absolute path) is silently NOT honored by
  CPython 3.14's `site.py` when the project path contains a space (`AI Projects`) → console
  script raises `ModuleNotFoundError`. `uv run python -c` works (uv injects path) but the
  installed bin doesn't. `python -m` from repo root puts CWD on `sys.path[0]` → flat-layout
  package imports directly, no editable/.pth needed. `pocket_llm/__main__.py` enables it.
  Layout is flat (`pocket_llm/` at root, NOT `src/`).
- `pocket_llm/engine.py`: `Engine` wraps `llama_cpp.Llama`; `stream()` yields
  chat chunks, `generate()` returns `GenMetrics` (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens≈chunks,
  ttft_s, total_s, peak_rss_mb via `ru_maxrss`; `tokens_per_s` property). `n_gpu_layers=-1`
  = all layers on Metal.
- `pocket_llm/cli.py`: argparse — one-shot `pocket-llm "prompt"` or REPL; footer
  `[ N tok | X tok/s | TTFT ms | peak RAM MB ]` to stderr.
- `scripts/download_model.py`: `hf_hub_download` → `./models/`. Default
  `unsloth/Qwen3.5-4B-GGUF :: Qwen3.5-4B-Q4_K_M.gguf` (~2.6GB). Default `--n-ctx` 16384
  (Qwen3.5 = 262K native / 1M via YaRN, but KV cache ~140KB/tok caps usable ctx ~16-32K on 8GB).
- `models/` + `*.gguf` gitignored (never commit weights).

## Status

- **Verified end-to-end** on the user's M-series Mac: `uv sync` built `llama-cpp-python==0.3.29`
  (Metal). Default now **Qwen3.5-4B Q4_K_M** (~2.6GB, no 404); old Qwen2.5-3B gguf deleted.
  Real numbers via `python -m pocket_llm` @ n_ctx 16384: **Qwen3.5-4B ~35.8 tok/s, peak RAM
  ~3.2 GB, TTFT ~270 ms** (earlier Qwen2.5-3B was ~57–58 tok/s / ~2.3GB). Well under 8GB.
  100% free (Apache-2.0 weights, MIT llama.cpp, local/offline, zero API tokens).
- `completion_tokens` counts stream chunks (approx tokens).

## CCR integration (no new CLI)

`pocket_llm/serve.py` → `uv run python -m pocket_llm.serve` exposes the model over an
**OpenAI-compatible** API (`127.0.0.1:8080/v1`, model id `qwen3.5-4b-local`), wrapping
`llama-cpp-python[server]`. Registered as the `local` provider in CCR (see [[ccr-openrouter]]). CCR routes
**per-request** on the model field → a local session (`qwen` = `command ccr code
--model "local,qwen3.5-4b-local"`) runs in PARALLEL with cloud `ccr code`, no global
switch / no 2nd instance. Files isolated per session via `work` (git worktree).
Verified: `/v1/chat/completions` returns a completion. NOT added to the `fallback-models`
transformer (would overwrite the model id). Claude Code (official) can't use local models
directly — CCR is the only integration point.

**End-to-end CCR routing VERIFIED** (2026-06-15): request to CCR's Anthropic endpoint
(`127.0.0.1:3456/v1/messages`) with `Router.default='local,qwen3.5-4b-local'` → routed
through local provider → llama server → reply `pong`, response `model=qwen3.5-4b-local`.
Default restored to OpenRouter afterwards (local is opt-in via `ccr-local`).

## Serve stability — DO NOT re-enable flash_attn/cache/big batch

Qwen3.5-4B is a **hybrid SSM/attention** model. `--flash_attn true` triggers a Metal
kernel-compilation storm → server crashes (empty reply, port held by orphan
`llama_cpp.server` child). `--n_batch 2048 + --cache true` together also crash it.
**Verified-stable serve config**: `n_ctx 32768`, `n_batch 1024`, no flash_attn, no cache
(committed in `serve.py`, exposes `--n-batch`; 2048 only crashes WITH flash+cache). Bench:
floor latency 0.9s, 7019-token prefill ~40s (~176 tok/s), server stays UP. Prefill scales
linearly → Claude-Code's ~26k-token/turn context ≈ ~150s/turn (4B prefill is physics, not a
bug). The earlier "non risponde quasi mai" was the flash/cache crash + content-array 500
([[ccr-openrouter]] `local-content-fix`), now both fixed. Kill BOTH on stop:
`pkill -f pocket_llm.serve; pkill -f llama_cpp.server`.

**Two UX gotchas (not server bugs):** (1) llama-cpp-python serves ONE request at a time —
a big in-flight turn blocks new ones; Claude Code's UI interrupt does NOT cancel server-side
inference (HTTP disconnect ignored), so a stuck turn keeps the slot busy → restart server to
clear it. (2) Biggest user-side speed lever is `/effort low` in the session (medium makes a
4B emit long reasoning → minutes). RAM is NOT a lever: model is 3.2GB, fully on Metal,
compute-bound not memory-bound — adding RAM does nothing. Local = offline/simple; fast
interactive work = cloud.

## DECISION (2026-06-15): local = offline/experiment only; interactive = cloud

Settled after the user hit ~2min/turn on a plain "ciao" in Claude Code. Proof: the RAW
model answers "ciao" in **0.36s**; the same "ciao" via Claude Code/CCR carries ~26k tokens
of system+tools harness → ~150s prefill, and the hybrid SSM can't cache it. No param fixes
this — it's structural. **User chose: keep local installed for offline/test, do all
interactive work on cloud (Claude Code Opus + CCR→OpenRouter, already the default).** Do NOT
re-pitch local-in-Claude-Code as "fast"; it is reliable, not fast. Local server is normally
STOPPED (relaunch via the VS Code extension only when offline). A stuck llama_cpp.server
ignores SIGTERM mid-Metal-compute → needs `kill -9`.

## VS Code extension = native chat webview (v0.0.6, model-agnostic)

**Lives in `pocket-llm/vscode-extension/`** (RaggioS/pocket-llm@main — MOVED here from
claude-config so the whole product is one repo; deploy: `cp -R vscode-extension ~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher` + Reload Window). Now v0.0.16. Big webview script is `vscode-extension/media/webview.js` (real file via asWebviewUri — avoids template-literal escaping). Features added: streaming SSE, stop-gen, multi-session panels (each launch = new panel/session), in-panel history overlay (open/delete), markdown + code blocks (copy/open-editor/save-file), personas + "about me" profile + temp/max_tokens (globalState), text/code file context, image input. Live copy `~/.vscode/extensions/local-ai-launcher/`.
Repointed from `ccr code --model local` (Claude Code harness, ~26k tok/turn, minutes) to a
**native webview chat** that POSTs DIRECTLY to `127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions` via
extension↔webview message-passing (no CCR, no harness) → ~2s replies, offline, zero token.
Button/status-bar `localAi.launch` opens the panel; auto-starts server + polls readiness;
stop kills both processes. **Model-agnostic**: UI says "Local AI", the model is only in
setting `localAi.modelId` (swap a future better local model with one setting, no rename).
Other settings: `serverUrl`, `serveDir`, `maxTokens` (512), `systemPrompt` (distilled house
rules — do NOT paste full CLAUDE.md/memories here, it re-bloats context and kills speed).
What CANNOT carry to a raw chat: hooks, skills, status-line (Claude Code machinery); only
CLAUDE.md/memory *content* can, as a short system prompt. Bump version to force VS Code reload.

## MULTIMODAL (2026-06-15): Qwen3-VL-8B via llama-server, text + images

Local chat is now **multimodal**. Model swapped from text-only Qwen3.5-4B to **Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct
Q4_K_M** (`models/qwen3-vl-8b/`, ~4.7GB + mmproj-F16 ~1.1GB). KEY: `llama-cpp-python` does NOT
support Qwen3-VL → serve via llama.cpp's native **`llama-server`** (`brew install llama.cpp`,
binary at `/opt/homebrew/bin/llama-server`, also `llama-mtmd-cli`). New wrapper
`pocket_llm/serve_vision.py` runs `llama-server -m <model> --mmproj <proj> -ngl 99 -c 8192 --jinja`,
OpenAI-compatible on :8080, model id `qwen3-vl-8b-local`. Accepts `image_url` content parts
(data URLs). Verified e2e: text ~1s/27 tok/s, image (red square → "Rosso") ~0.6s, server UP.
Extension (v0.0.9) starts `serve_vision`, panel has image input (attach 📎 / paste / drag-drop,
thumbnails, bubbles render images, history persists multimodal msgs). stopServer kills
`serve_vision`+`llama-server`. Old text path (`serve.py`, Qwen3.5-4B gguf) still on disk as
fallback, now superseded.

## Bench + UX polish (2026-06-15, panel v0.0.11)

Bench Qwen3-VL-8B (M-series, 17GB): RAM RSS **4.1GB**; gen **~25 tok/s** costante (corto=lungo);
TTFT testo corto 54–464ms (caldo ~50ms); prefill grande ~3700 tok = **TTFT 12.7s** (collo
bottiglia); immagine 64px 0.6s, immagine reale 1024×768 **4.5s**. **1 solo slot** → richieste
concorrenti serializzano. Panel ora: **streaming SSE** (postChatStream, token live), **bottone
Stop** (Invia→Stop rosso durante gen, aborta req, tiene parziale), **indicatore prefill pulsante**
(bolla "…" fade, copre l'attesa muta immagini grandi). maxTokens setting default 512.

## Status: COMPLETE & shipped

Repo `RaggioS/pocket-llm` (private). Full reproducible README (Requirements,
install-on-another-device, GPU backends, CCR integration + toggle, troubleshooting, tests),
LICENSE (MIT), smoke tests green (`uv run --extra dev pytest`). Engine + CLI + REPL + serve,
default Qwen3.5-4B Q4. CCR local provider live + e2e-verified. Nothing pending to ship.

## Roadmap (optional future)

Bench harness (sweep Q2/Q4/Q5/Q8 → tok/s vs RAM vs quality), "does it fit?" pre-flight
(file size + free RAM before load), CPU-only mode bench for low-end/Windows machines.
- **Richer Hermes prefill — DONE & validated 2026-06-23**: added a "Per-project quick facts" section to
  `HERMES.md` (run commands + key gotchas per project) so Hermes answers grounded questions without runtime
  discovery tool-calls. Measured: HERMES.md 1592→2477 B, +232 prompt tokens, **warm prefill +8ms** (172→181ms;
  prompt-cache absorbs it), cold +845ms once per session. Grounding verified: "how to run pocket-llm" →
  `uv run python -m pocket_llm`, no tools, top behavioral rules NOT diluted. Verdict: worth it, kept. Sweet
  spot reached — pushing further adds cold cost + dilution risk for little gain. Method for future tweaks:
  measure warm `prompt_eval_duration` x2 (2nd = cached) before/after; do NOT dump CLAUDE.md/memories (re-bloats).

**Triggers:** pocket-llm, quantized local LLM, Qwen 3B Q4, llama-cpp-python, 8GB LLM, GGUF, tok/s bench, local inference PoC
**Why:** new ongoing project outliving the task; documents the symlink shared-config pattern reused by future repos.
**How to apply:** next session on this repo, resume from roadmap; for a NEW separate project replicate the symlink+gitignore pattern above. Use `( cd pocket-llm && ... )` subshell per [[bash-cwd-hooks]].

# jcode (RaggioS fork) — operational reference

Our personal fork of [1jehuang/jcode](https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode) = the **sole local-gemma4 coding
harness** (replaced Hermes for that lane 2026-06-25; eval → [[jcode-eval]]). MIT, Rust. Upstream is a
single-author project; we own the fork so our patches are durable + rebase-able.

## Repos / locations
- Fork: `https://github.com/RaggioS/jcode` (remote `origin`); upstream `https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode` (remote `upstream`).
- Working clone: **`$WS/jcode`** (git-ignored by `$WS` ignore-all, own remote — same pattern as pocket-llm/ccr-free-router).
- Branch with our changes: **`feat/local-gemma4-macos`** (PR #1 on the fork). Rebase this on `upstream/master`.
- Installed binary: `~/.local/bin/jcode` → `~/.jcode/builds/current/jcode` (via `scripts/install_release.sh`).
- App data/config: `~/.jcode/` (config.toml, logs, sessions, skills, mcp.json). Global persona: `~/AGENTS.md`.

## Build / install / update
```bash
cd "$WS/jcode"
PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" cargo build --release --bin jcode   # ~5 min cold, ~10s incremental
scripts/install_release.sh                                          # → ~/.local/bin/jcode
# update from upstream:
git fetch upstream && git rebase upstream/master   # on feat/local-gemma4-macos, then rebuild+install
```
Rust installed via `brew install rust` (1.96). Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in PATH.

## Our patches (vs upstream) — see fork `PATCHES.md`
- **macOS image paste on non-English locales** (`crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/{helpers.rs,input.rs}`):
  - `clipboard_image()`: AppleScript-ObjC → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`). AppleScript dialect errored on
    Italian locale (`-2741 found plural class name`) → silent image-paste failure. JXA is locale-independent.
  - `handle_paste()`: empty bracketed paste (macOS/VS Code Cmd+V with image-only clipboard = 0-char paste) →
    read clipboard image + attach. Cmd+V AND Ctrl+V now attach screenshots. Good upstream-PR candidate.

## Runtime config (machine-local; canonical templates in `pocket-llm/jcode/`)
- **Thinking off** for gemma4: Ollama `/v1` honors top-level `reasoning_effort`. Set in `~/.jcode/config.toml`
  (`[providers.ollama-local] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`) and/or env
  `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` (the launcher sets the env).
- **Telemetry off**: `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (jcode collects anonymous stats by default).
- **Italian persona**: `~/AGENTS.md` (global, loaded into every system prompt) + project `AGENTS.md`.
- **Run local**: `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` (TUI) — no login/key (Ollama = local endpoint).
- **Swarm**: implemented (`jcode-swarm-core` channels/DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch`). Caveat: N gemma4
  agents on one Mac serialize on the GPU/Ollama → for real concurrency point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.

## Launcher
pocket-llm VS Code extension (robot button) launches jcode for the gemma4 lane → [[pocket-llm]].

- Installed binary: `~/.local/bin/jcode` → `~/.jcode/builds/current/jcode` (via `scripts/install_release.sh`).
- App data/config: `~/.jcode/` (config.toml, logs, sessions, skills, mcp.json). Global persona: `~/AGENTS.md`.

## Build / install / update
```bash
cd "$WS/jcode"
PATH="/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" cargo build --release --bin jcode   # ~5 min cold, ~10s incremental
scripts/install_release.sh                                          # → ~/.local/bin/jcode
# update from upstream:
git fetch upstream && git rebase upstream/master   # on feat/local-gemma4-macos, then rebuild+install
```
Rust installed via `brew install rust` (1.96). Ensure `~/.local/bin` precedes `~/.cargo/bin` in PATH.

## Our patches (vs upstream) — see fork `PATCHES.md`
- **macOS image paste on non-English locales** (`crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/{helpers.rs,input.rs}`):
  - `clipboard_image()`: AppleScript-ObjC → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`). AppleScript dialect errored on
    Italian locale (`-2741 found plural class name`) → silent image-paste failure. JXA is locale-independent.
  - `handle_paste()`: empty bracketed paste (macOS/VS Code Cmd+V with image-only clipboard = 0-char paste) →
    read clipboard image + attach. Cmd+V AND Ctrl+V now attach screenshots. Good upstream-PR candidate.

## Runtime config (machine-local; canonical templates in `pocket-llm/jcode/`)
- **Thinking off** for gemma4: Ollama `/v1` honors top-level `reasoning_effort`. Set in `~/.jcode/config.toml`
  (`[providers.ollama-local] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`) and/or env
  `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` (the launcher sets the env).
- **Telemetry off**: `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (jcode collects anonymous stats by default).
- **Italian persona**: `~/AGENTS.md` (global, loaded into every system prompt) + project `AGENTS.md`.
- **Run local**: `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b` (TUI) — no login/key (Ollama = local endpoint).
- **Swarm**: implemented (`jcode-swarm-core` channels/DM/broadcast + `jcode-terminal-launch`). Caveat: N gemma4
  agents on one Mac serialize on the GPU/Ollama → for real concurrency point swarm workers at OpenRouter free.

## Launcher
pocket-llm VS Code extension (robot button) launches jcode for the gemma4 lane → [[pocket-llm]].

[[jcode-eval]] [[pocket-llm]] [[hermes-agent-docs]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode fork, RaggioS jcode, build jcode, install jcode, jcode config, jcode thinking off, jcode image paste, jcode swarm, rebase jcode upstream, local gemma4 harness
**Why:** jcode is now the daily local-gemma4 coding harness; the operational details (build/install/config/patches) must not be re-derived each session.
**How to apply:** for any local-gemma4 jcode task, work in `$WS/jcode` on `feat/local-gemma4-macos`; rebuild+install after changes; rebase from upstream periodically; config templates live in `pocket-llm/jcode/`.

---
name: jcode-eval
description: Evaluating jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) as a lighter replacement for Hermes driving local gemma4. In-flight experiment + Hermes rollback baseline.
metadata:
  type: project
---

# jcode evaluation — candidate lighter Hermes replacement

Goal (set 2026-06-25): test whether **jcode** can replace **Hermes** as the daily local-model
coding harness, driving `gemma4:12b` on Ollama (or a future local model). Hypothesis: jcode is
much lighter/faster (Rust, ~27 MB RAM embedding-off vs Hermes' Python/Node stack), so response
and eval latency should improve. Evaluate REAL impact before committing.

## What jcode is
- Repo: https://github.com/1jehuang/jcode — MIT, single-author (Jeremy Huang), very active.
- Rust coding-agent harness (Codex/Claude-Code category), NOT an LLM. BYO-model: ~40 OpenAI-compatible
  providers + native Claude/OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter. Free path = local Ollama / LM Studio / OpenRouter `:free`.
- Ollama profile: api_base `http://localhost:11434/v1`, auth "local endpoint" (no key), default_model none
  → must pass `--model gemma4:12b`. Login: `jcode login --provider ollama` then
  `jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '<prompt>'`.
- Differentiators vs Hermes: single-server multi-client daemon (Unix socket, hot-reload), semantic
  memory (local all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings + petgraph), swarm (multi-agent same repo), MCP + hooks.
- Maturity caveats: ~277k LOC, **1258 prod `unwrap`/`expect`** (panic risk), 50 files >1200 LOC,
  version mismatch (Cargo 0.31.2 vs badge v0.9.1888-dev), no real stable release, bus factor 1.
  → fine as personal experiment, risky for "prod" reliance.

## Experiment state (2026-06-25)
- Clone: scratchpad temp only (`.../scratchpad/jcode`), NOT in `$WS`. 503 MB.
- Installed **Rust 1.96.0 via Homebrew** (`brew install rust`, pulled `pkgconf` dep) — was absent.
  Removable via `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` if abandoning.
- Build: `cargo build --release --bin jcode` → OK 4m56s, 94 MB binary (`target/release/jcode`).
- Telemetry: jcode collects anonymous stats by default → opt out with `DO_NOT_TRACK=1` (also `JCODE_NO_TELEMETRY`).
- **Smoke-test PASSED** (2026-06-25): `DO_NOT_TRACK=1 jcode --provider ollama --model gemma4:12b run '...'`
  works end-to-end; `ollama ps` confirms gemma4 loaded 100% GPU, jcode sets num_ctx 16384. No `jcode login`
  needed for ollama (local endpoint, no key).
- **Latency finding**: warm one-liner = **~11s**, cold = ~65s. BUT jcode runs gemma4 with **thinking ON by
  default** (emits `💭` reasoning even for trivial prompts) → that's the slowness, NOT harness weight.
  jcode HAS the lever (`[provider] openai_reasoning_effort` + per-provider `supports_reasoning_effort`,
  provider-core `set_reasoning_effort`) but does NOT send reasoning_effort=none to ollama by default →
  same trap Hermes already fixed (Ollama /v1 honors `reasoning_effort`, ignores `think`). NOT yet tuned in jcode.
- **THINKING-OFF TUNING (works, 2026-06-25)**: `JCODE_OPENAI_EXTRA_BODY='{"reasoning_effort":"none"}'` env
  injects the field into the ollama /v1 body → gemma4 thinking OFF (zero `💭`, code only). Same lever Hermes
  uses. To persist for real use: bake into config.toml `[providers.<name>] extra_body = { reasoning_effort = "none" }`
  on a named ollama provider, or export the env in the launcher. Result warm: thinking-ON 11s → **thinking-OFF 8s**
  (stable ×2); cold ~60s. The 8s is one-shot `jcode run` (re-pays server connect + prompt-eval each call);
  interactive TUI on the persistent warm server would feel snappier.
- **KEY VERDICT**: same model + same Ollama = same decode floor (~7.6 tok/s) regardless of harness. Tuned
  thinking-off, jcode ≈ Hermes on gemma4 speed (both model-bound) — jcode TIES, never beats. So the switch
  decision is NOT about speed; it's about jcode's other edges (RAM ~27 MB vs Hermes Python/Node, boot/UI,
  semantic memory, swarm) vs Hermes' maturity + already-tuned setup. If faster gemma4 is the real goal, the
  lever is a smaller model, not the harness.
- **NO Hermes cutover done yet.** Hermes fully intact and primary.

## Image input (vision) — gemma4 OK, jcode macOS attach BROKEN (2026-06-25)
- gemma4:12b Ollama capabilities = completion, **vision**, audio, tools, thinking (clip projector). Vision REAL:
  direct `POST /v1/chat/completions` with `image_url` data-uri described the screenshot correctly in **4s**.
  So model + OpenAI image protocol both fine.
- jcode TUI image input = clipboard paste only (no `--image` flag on `run`). macOS read path
  (`jcode-tui/.../helpers.rs::clipboard_image`, `#[cfg(macos)]`) shells an embedded **AppleScript**
  (NSPasteboard→PNG). On this **Italian-locale** Mac that AppleScript **errors** ("nome classe plurale",
  -2741) → returns nothing → falls back to arboard (unverified). Net: pasting a fresh screenshot does NOT
  attach → user sees "doesn't accept images". Also terminals can hijack Cmd/Super+V (paste = the keybind).
- Verdict: image input is a real macOS friction/bug for our setup, NOT a gemma4 limit. Fixable (patch the
  applescript / rely on arboard / add a `run --image` flag) but = single-author immaturity tax. Workaround
  today: an image URL pastes/downloads; local screenshots have no good jcode path yet.
- **FIX APPLIED (2026-06-25)**: rewrote the macOS `clipboard_image()` branch in
  `crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/helpers.rs` from AppleScript-ObjC → **JXA** (`osascript -l JavaScript`,
  NSPasteboard→NSImage→PNG type 4). JXA syntax is locale-independent → no more -2741. Verified standalone:
  extracts a clipboard screenshot to valid PNG. Rebuilt (incremental 7.9s), JXA marker baked in binary.
  **Trigger is Ctrl+V, NOT Cmd+V**: `is_clipboard_paste_shortcut` = `v/V` + CONTROL|ALT|SUPER|META, but
  macOS terminals hijack Cmd+V (paste text, image dropped via bracketed paste → `handle_paste` skips
  clipboard_image by design). Ctrl+V reaches jcode → smart paste → JXA → image attaches. Patch is a local
  scratchpad edit (not committed upstream); lost if scratchpad cleaned or repo re-cloned.
- **FIX #2 — Cmd+V (the one the user actually wants), 2026-06-25**: user reported Cmd+V attaches images in
  EVERY other agent in the same VS Code terminal, only jcode failed → so the terminal does NOT eat Cmd+V.
  jcode logs (`~/.jcode/logs/jcode-*.log`) proved it: Cmd+V with an image emits `Text paste: 0 chars, 0 lines`
  — VS Code delivers an EMPTY bracketed paste, and jcode's `handle_paste` (input.rs) deliberately skipped
  `clipboard_image()` on bracketed pastes (old Wayland multi-MIME false-positive guard) → image dropped.
  Other agents read the clipboard image on an empty paste; jcode didn't. **Fix**: in `handle_paste`, when
  `text.is_empty()` → `spawn_clipboard_paste(ImageOnly)` (reads clipboard image via the JXA path, attaches).
  Safe because it only fires on EMPTY text (nothing to misidentify); non-empty pastes keep the old guard.
  → Cmd+V now works like every other agent; Ctrl+V still works too. NO keybindings.json / terminal remap
  needed (that idea was wrong — left untouched). Both patches are local scratchpad edits; good upstream-PR
  candidates (helpers.rs JXA + input.rs empty-paste image).

## mermaid-rs-renderer (separate repo, asked 2026-06-25)
https://github.com/1jehuang/mermaid-rs-renderer — same author, standalone Rust mermaid renderer (no browser/TS,
~1800× faster). Already an OPTIONAL git dep of jcode (`jcode-tui-mermaid` feature `renderer`, tag v0.2.1, default
OFF → our build excluded it). Not needed separately for the jcode eval; only worth adopting standalone if we want
fast mermaid rendering in OTHER tools (Hermes/pocket-llm/a CLI). Noted, not actioned.

## Rollback baseline — Hermes (DO NOT switch until eval passes + user OK)
Current Hermes = the thing jcode would replace. See [[hermes-agent-docs]] for full config. Key points:
- Home `~/.hermes`: `config.yaml`, `SOUL.md`, profiles, source checkout `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`.
- Two profiles: `default` → gemma4:12b local (Chip / pocket-llm ext); `openrouter` → agy/Gemini (FreeCode).
- Launchers: `freecode` in `ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`; VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  vscode-launcher) runs `freecode`. The real cutover = repoint these launchers/button from Hermes to jcode.
- **SCOPE CONSTRAINT (user, 2026-06-25): cutover ONLY the local gemma4 lane** (Hermes `default` profile /
  Chip / pocket-llm). The **Free Router lane MUST NOT be touched** (`openrouter` profile / agy/Gemini /
  freecode / robot button stay on Hermes). jcode replaces ONLY the gemma4-local agent.
- Source patch (thinking/temp) in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` via pocket-llm reapply script.
- **Before any cutover**: back up `~/.hermes/config.yaml` (+ profiles) and snapshot launcher files so we
  can revert to Hermes if jcode underperforms.

[[hermes-agent-docs]] [[pocket-llm]] [[ccr-free-router-repo]] [[toolchain-llm-clis]]

**Triggers:** jcode, jcode eval, replace hermes, lighter harness, gemma4 jcode, ollama jcode, rust coding agent, 1jehuang, cutover hermes, rollback hermes
**Why:** user wants to A/B jcode vs Hermes for the local coding lane; needs a clean revert path if jcode loses.
**How to apply:** continue the experiment from "Experiment state"; never cutover launchers without backing up `~/.hermes` first + explicit user approval. If abandoning jcode, `brew uninstall rust pkgconf` + delete scratchpad clone.

---
name: hermes-agent-docs
description: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — docs, repo, local source checkout, key CLI/slash commands for our local-Ollama setup.
metadata:
  type: reference
---

# Hermes Agent — docs & pointers

Agent powering pocket-llm's local coding TUI (see [[pocket-llm]]). MIT, by Nous Research.

## Links
- Repo: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
- Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/ (quickstart, CLI, configuration, tools, skills, memory, MCP, cron, context-files, architecture, env-vars)
- Install one-liner: `curl -fsSL https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/install.sh | bash`

## Local install (authoritative for OUR installed version — read this, not the web, offline + 0 chat tokens)
- Home: `~/.hermes` (config.yaml, .env, SOUL.md, sessions DB, hooks, skills).
- **Source git checkout**: `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` — the SAME tree `hermes update` pulls. Any local
  source patch (e.g. customizing `/resume`) is OVERWRITTEN on update → avoid patching; prefer config/flags.
- TUI vs classic: `hermes --tui` (modern TUI, interactive picker overlays) vs `hermes --cli` (classic REPL).
  Also set via `display.interface: tui` in config.yaml. `_parser.py` defines both flags.

## Key commands / slash
- CLI: `hermes` (chat), `hermes model`, `hermes tools`, `hermes skills`, `hermes sessions`, `hermes config set`,
  `hermes doctor` (diagnose), `hermes update`, `hermes prompt-size` (STATIC max budget, not live prefill).
- Slash: `/model`, `/personality [name]`, `/new`·`/reset`, `/retry`·`/undo`, `/compress`, `/usage`,
  `/insights [--days N]`, `/skills`, `/resume <number|id|title>` (bare `/resume` lists → type just the number).
- Interrupt: `Ctrl+C` or send a new message.
- Context files (cwd, prompt_builder.py `build_context_files_prompt`): loads ONE, first-found by priority
  `HERMES.md`/`.hermes.md` (walk to git root) > `AGENTS.md` > `CLAUDE.md` > `.cursorrules`, PLUS `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` always.
  All are PREFILL. Workspace root `HERMES.md` (tracked via `git add -f`; ignore-all repo) is our Hermes-only context:
  tuned persona (Italian, concise/aseptic/functional, speed>verbosity, one line before multi-step actions) + active
  projects. It SHADOWS the caveman `AGENTS.md` and the 8KB `CLAUDE.md` for Hermes (neither reaches Hermes).
  `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` now carries a MINIMAL global Italian-default persona (Reply in Italian by default, concise/
  aseptic, switch only on user cue) — because HERMES.md only loads when the cwd (up to its git root) has it, so
  opening any OTHER project would drop the Italian directive; SOUL.md is always loaded, so it guarantees the default
  everywhere (set 2026-06-23, supersedes the earlier "leave SOUL.md empty" note). Kept minimal to avoid duplicating
  the fuller HERMES.md persona. SOUL.md reloads fresh each message (no restart). It now also adds grounding
  (read/use tools before acting, don't invent file contents/APIs), work hygiene (only the lines asked for, verify
  before closing, 127.0.0.1), safety (destructive→confirm), and Italian-orthography (accents, never ASCII). The
  richer distributed persona lives in `pocket-llm/hermes/SOUL.md` (a template, aligned to this lean version via
  PR #12 2026-06-23). **SOUL.md ≠ CLAUDE.md**: SOUL.md is the always-on persona (prefill EVERY message → keep lean);
  the real CLAUDE.md analog is the per-cwd `HERMES.md` (verbose project rules, loaded only in that repo).
  Loader path (verified 2026-06-23): `agent/prompt_builder.py::load_soul_md` reads `~/.hermes/SOUL.md`, `.strip()`,
  if non-empty returns it → `agent/system_prompt.py:155-157` appends to `stable_parts` = system-prompt identity slot #1.
  Verify it's actually loaded by running Hermes' OWN function (system python lacks `yaml` — use the Hermes venv):
  `~/.hermes/hermes-agent/venv/bin/python -c "import sys;sys.path.insert(0,'.');from agent.prompt_builder import load_soul_md;print(len(load_soul_md(16384) or ''))"`
  (run from `~/.hermes/hermes-agent`; non-zero len = loaded). Confirmed: 849 chars, Italian+grounding+safety all present.
  Don't edit CLAUDE.md (shared brain) or AGENTS.md (generic, other agents).

## What we deliberately disable for prefill/speed (all re-activatable — see pocket-llm/vscode-extension/HERMES.md)
Memory learning-loop, agent-curated memory, FTS5 session search, skills (71 removed), delegation/subagents,
gateway (Telegram/Discord/Slack), cron, vision/tts/browser/image_gen tools. README's headline features =
exactly these; our build trades the self-improving loop for a snappy local agent.

## DON'T re-attempt: TUI status-bar customization (settled 2026-06-18)
The `--tui` status bar (`model/ctx/timer · 📁cwd (branch)`) is HARDCODED in the compiled React TUI
(`ui-tui`, cwd/branch from `fmtCwdBranch` in `ui-tui/src/domain/paths.ts`, rendered by
`components/appChrome.tsx`). There is NO supported config/hook/plugin API to change its layout — open
feature requests confirm it: NousResearch/hermes-agent#13490, #17540, #17543. The only way is to patch
core source + `npm run build` the bundle, which `hermes update` reverts (a fragile workaround). We TRIED
it (worked: `📁 …/AI Projects ⎇ master`) then REVERTED — user wants native-or-nothing, no forced
workaround. Native bar already shows folder+branch. `gemma4:12b` ctx shows model max (262.1k) not our
runtime `num_ctx` — cosmetic. If a future Hermes adds `/statusbar config` (the requested feature), use that.

## Switching the local model — provider is `custom`, NOT `ollama` (settled 2026-06-23)
A local Ollama OpenAI-compatible endpoint is a **`custom`** provider to Hermes. Labeling it `provider: ollama`
in config.yaml logs `Unknown provider 'ollama'` and silently FALLS BACK to auto-detection / the previously
cached model. Working block:
```yaml
model:
  default: <ollama-tag>      # e.g. gemma4:12b
  provider: custom
  api_key: ollama            # any non-empty string; Ollama ignores it
  base_url: http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1
```
**Editing `model.default` alone is NOT enough**: Hermes validates the tag against its model-picker disk cache
(`~/.hermes/provider_models_cache.json`, `context_length_cache.yaml`); a tag absent from cache → fallback to the
old model (verified: a pulled-but-uncached tag ran the previous model despite the config edit). Fix via
`hermes model` (interactive, needs a TTY): pick the saved `custom`/`Local (127.0.0.1:11434)` provider → it
re-fetches live `/v1/models` → select the tag. `hermes model --refresh` wipes+rebuilds the cache. After an
`ollama pull` of a NEW tag, re-run the picker so Hermes sees it. The picker also rewrites config.yaml
(alphabetizes keys, adds a `custom_providers:` entry). `hermes model` cannot be run non-interactively (errors
"requires an interactive terminal") → guide the user to run it.

## TWO SEPARATE LANES via Hermes PROFILES (2026-06-24, supersedes "single shared default = agy")
Two independent agents that must NOT step on each other: **local gemma4** (Chip / pocket-llm extension) vs
**cloud Gemini+router** (FreeCode / ccr-free-router robot button). Earlier both ran plain `hermes` → ONE shared
`~/.hermes/config.yaml`, ONE default → whichever launched LAST won the default (a launch race even made one fall
back to gemma4 while the other kept agy). They also couldn't have different prefill weights (gemma4 needs a LEAN
prefill). **FIX = Hermes' native `profile` feature** (`hermes profile create/list/use/alias`; each profile =
`~/.hermes/profiles/<name>/` with its OWN config.yaml + SOUL.md + skills; the shared CODE/plugin patch stays in
`~/.hermes/hermes-agent`, so the thinking/temperature patch covers both lanes automatically):
- **`default` profile** (`~/.hermes`) → **gemma4:12b** local (Ollama :11434). LEAN prefill (no bundled skills).
  Launched by the **Chip** pocket-llm extension's plain `hermes --tui` — pocket-llm untouched.
- **`openrouter` profile** (`~/.hermes/profiles/openrouter`, wrapper cmd `~/.local/bin/openrouter`) → **agy /
  Gemini 3.5 Flash** (agy shim :3457) + router. Richer prefill (71 bundled skills). Launched by **freecode**,
  which now runs `openrouter --tui` (ccr-free-router PR #1, falls back to `hermes`); the robot button runs freecode.
- Created via `hermes profile create openrouter`; its config seeded by copying the working agy `config.yaml`, then
  the `default` config flipped back to gemma4. Name is model-agnostic ("openrouter" — today gemini+OpenRouter free,
  could be other models later). Per-profile `SOUL.md`/`HERMES.md` = where you tune each lane's persona/prefill.
  Both profiles' `SOUL.md` carry the SAME lean Italian brevity backbone (concise/direct, expand only when asked,
  code complete, grounding, safety) — 2026-06-24 — with distinct framing (local coder vs cloud daily-driver):
  "one agent body, two profile souls". Each soul's TEMPLATE is tracked in its lane's repo for reproducibility:
  gemma4 → `pocket-llm/hermes/SOUL.md`; Gemini → `ccr-free-router/hermes/SOUL.md` (PR #2). Live files:
  `~/.hermes/SOUL.md` + `~/.hermes/profiles/openrouter/SOUL.md` (untracked). NB the real gemma4 speed lever is
  OUTPUT brevity (decode-bound ~7.6 tok/s), NOT prefill size (prompt-eval ~7300 tok/s = near-free), so "richer
  prefill for the cloud lane" was dropped; both stay lean.
Verified: `hermes status` → gemma4:12b · `openrouter status` → Gemini 3.5 Flash (Medium); `hermes profile list`
shows both. Old single-default note (the agy shim still wraps Antigravity CLI `agy`; `GET /v1/models` added in
commit `4fe24c0`; backups `config.yaml.pre-agy.bak`/`.pre-separate.bak`) now applies only to the `openrouter` lane.
- **Launcher**: `freecode` lives in **`ccr-free-router/zshrc-helpers.sh`** (tracked, sourced by `~/.zshrc`; NOT
  inlined) = guard-if-no-`hermes` + `_ccr_shim_up` + `hermes --tui`. The VS Code **robot button** (ccr-free-router
  `vscode-launcher`, v0.1.0) now launches `freecode` too (shellArgs `-lic freecode`, prewarms the agy shim, status
  bar reads `agy-state.json`, menu "Switch HERMES model"→`hermes model`) — terminal and button behave identically.
  User decision: ALL the launcher orchestration goes in ccr-free-router; do NOT touch the pocket-llm repo/extension
  (that's a separate gemma4-local project). The intended replacement for ever typing `claude`/`ccr code` daily.
- **Caveat (same wall as ccr code)**: the shim runs `agy -p` = TEXT-ONLY, no native `tool_use`. So Hermes'
  agentic tool-loop over agy is degraded (chat/simple turns fine; gemma4 local DOES emit tool_calls via Ollama
  → switch to it via `hermes model` for tool-heavy work). agy ~7-15s/call. To restore failover/tools, switch
  the primary back to gemma4 or point at CCR :3456.

## gemma4 thinking control — SOLVED via plugin patch (2026-06-23, supersedes "can't be disabled")
gemma4:12b is hybrid-thinking (Ollama capability `thinking`). Earlier it seemed unsuppressible; the real chain:
- Ollama's OpenAI **`/v1`** IGNORES the native `think` flag and `chat_template_kwargs`, but (Ollama ≥0.30.x)
  **HONORS `reasoning_effort`**: `none` → 0 reasoning (off), `low`/`medium`/`high` → thinking on. Verified by curl.
- Hermes' custom-provider plugin (`plugins/model-providers/custom/__init__.py`) emitted only `think:false`
  when effort=none → ineffective on `/v1`. And `_supports_reasoning_extra_body` (run_agent.py) returns False for
  any non-OpenRouter base_url, so Hermes never sent `reasoning_effort` to a local Ollama custom provider.
- System-prompt directives DON'T work (tested: gemma4 reasons ABOUT a "never think" rule instead of obeying).
**FIX (applied)**: patched `plugins/model-providers/custom/__init__.py` `build_api_kwargs_extras` to map the
reasoning effort onto Ollama's param: effort `none`/disabled → `extra_body["think"]=False` + `["reasoning_effort"]="none"`;
any other effort → `extra_body["reasoning_effort"]=<effort>`. Now the toggle works end-to-end:
- `/reasoning none` → thinking OFF (fast, direct) · `/reasoning low|medium|high` → thinking ON at that level.
- `/reasoning hide` still just hides the block from display (compute unaffected).
The SAME plugin patch ALSO sets balanced sampling (PR #14, 2026-06-24): returns `{"temperature":0.6,"top_p":0.9}`
as the TOP-LEVEL kwargs (2nd tuple element), not in `extra_body.options`. Why top-level: Ollama `/v1` honors
top-level OpenAI `temperature`/`top_p` (temp 0 → identical outputs) but IGNORES `options.temperature` (verified by
determinism test). gemma4's Ollama default temp is 1.0 (too high → rambly, less deterministic code); 0.6 is the
all-purpose middle ground (tighter code, still fine for chat). Returned as profile defaults so `request_overrides`
still win. Carried by the canonical copy + reapply script, so it survives `hermes update` together with the toggle.
**⚠ This is a SOURCE patch in `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (NOT a tracked repo) → `hermes update` OVERWRITES it.
Re-apply after every update** — but now AUTOMATED: the VS Code launcher runs the reapply script on every
launch BEFORE opening the TUI (pocket-llm PR #15, 2026-06-24, ext v0.1.1), so a fresh launch self-heals the
patch and the new process loads it — no manual re-apply, no "restart to activate" (the restart caveat only ever
applied because Python plugins import once at process start; launching IS the activation). Manual path stays a
ONE-COMMAND durable installer (pocket-llm, merged PR #11 2026-06-23):
`bash ~/Documents/AI\ Projects/pocket-llm/scripts/reapply-thinking-patch.sh` (idempotent: checks Ollama≥0.30,
skips if already patched, backs up live plugin, copies canonical `pocket-llm/hermes/custom_provider_patched.py`,
ast-validates). Default config now `agent.reasoning_effort: none` (thinking off). Exact change inside
`build_api_kwargs_extras`:
```python
if _effort == "none" or _enabled is False:
    extra_body["think"] = False
    extra_body["reasoning_effort"] = "none"   # added — Ollama /v1 honors this, not `think`
elif _effort:
    extra_body["reasoning_effort"] = _effort   # added — low/medium/high
```
Requires Ollama ≥0.30.x. Long-term: file/track upstream so the custom/Ollama provider sends `reasoning_effort`.

**Triggers:** hermes, hermes agent, nous research, hermes docs, hermes tui, hermes config, hermes update, hermes commands, hermes model, provider custom, ollama provider, unknown provider, model switch, model cache refresh, thinking, reasoning effort, /reasoning, disable thinking, gemma4 think
**Why:** the installed source is the source of truth (offline, version-exact); web README is cloud/Portal-oriented and mostly N/A to our local-Ollama trimmed build.
**How to apply:** for any Hermes question, read `~/.hermes/hermes-agent` (source) + `~/.hermes/config.yaml`; don't patch source (update overwrites) — change config/flags. Open docs URL only for conceptual guides.
